name: CF Industries Holdings
summary: Day 99 (Saturday June 6; covers 4 June afternoon -> 5 June US session -> 5-6 June). **CF $113.49 (-3.4% 1D) TRACKING THE UREA DE-PRICING / FERTILIZER PEACE-DIVIDEND.** CF Industries fell -3.4% to $113.49 and Nutrien -2.6% to $67.20 (-9.0% MoM) as the nitrogen-equity leg tracks the fast urea de-pricing — Middle East urea ~$416.50/T (-5.9% 1D) as the fertilizer complex prices the de-escalation/diplomacy track (the 5-6 June Gulf exchange was zero-casualty/calibrated; oil down on diplomacy). This is the fertilizer peace-dividend in equity form: the cascade path 2 (Qatar -> fertilizer -> India food security) leg de-pricing on deal-anticipation. Symmetric — CF snaps back UP fast if talks break. DISTINCT from the 5 June broad RATES/AI risk-off (gold -3.1%, oil -2%, VIX +39.7% on Broadcom miss + hot May jobs): CF's move here is the diplomacy/peace track, not the valuation/AI rout. PRIOR — Day 96 (Wednesday June 3; covers to 3 June morning). **CF +2.5% — THE QATAR-CASCADE FERTILIZER EQUITY LEG IS FINALLY BIDDING, AND DID SO ON A DAY THE ENTIRE METALS COMPLEX SOLD OFF.** CF Industries rose +2.5% to $116.31 (Nutrien +0.9% sympathy) on confirmation of the Qatar-urea-halt -> US-nitrogen-tightness mechanism in price action: QatarEnergy halted downstream urea when it cut LNG, urea is >$850/mt (April peak, +80% since Feb), US Gulf $516->$683/ton, NOLA +32% in a week. This is the equity manifestation of cascade path 2 (Qatar -> fertilizer -> India food security) firing in an attributable way — and a clean crisis-linked signal precisely because it bucked the broad metals/commodity selloff (markets E3). The fertilizer-urea -> cf-industries edge is strengthened (activation incremented). Reversibility LOW: Ras Laffan/QAFCO scarring is structural; India's import bill binds through the monsoon planting season. PRIOR — Day 92 (Saturday May 30; Friday 29 May close). **CF -11% MoM AS THE FERTILIZER WAR-PREMIUM CRACKS (UREA -35% MoM). CF Industries $112.35 (-3.6% 1D, -11.0% MoM, +8.2% 3M) and Nutrien (-7.5% MoM) de-priced the Qatar-disruption nitrogen premium hard on deal-anticipation + Modi's fertilizer-cut/import-restraint appeal -> Gulf urea export-normalization expectation. This node had gone stale (last updated 13 May); the -11% MoM unwind is now encoded. Reversible on a deal collapse / Gulf re-disruption; the slow physical legs (insurance, freight) have not unwound.** Created 2026-05-12. **C6 CF $130.39 MAY 13 (+4.7% 1D = DAY 2 COMPOUNDING; +12.9% cumulative over 2 sessions; +7.2% 1M; +37.7% 3M; +71% YTD).** Sector regime CONFIRMED 2nd consecutive session. The Modi-Araghchi-UAE-tour timing constellation is the MOST-ACTIONABLE NARRATIVE BRACKET OF CYCLE for fertilizer: (i) Iran FM Araghchi arrives Delhi May 13 for BRICS May 14-15, meets Modi May 14; (ii) Modi 5-nation tour CONFIRMED May 15-20 with UAE leg explicit focus on 'energy security for India amid US-Iran war' = direct government acknowledgement of fertilizer/import-cost binding; (iii) India publicly admitting it cannot solve import-cost problem; Modi flying to UAE means Indian state's first-best solution is bilateral Gulf supply — exactly the situation that keeps CF/Nutrien/BASF pricing power. Qatar/Ras Laffan offline since Feb 28; QAFCO/SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailment structural; ~35-40% of global urea export capacity affected. DAP India tender $935/t cfr May 11 extraordinary print; Urea US Gulf FOB futures ~$547.50/t (+50% vs pre-war); UBS PT $97→$140 with note that target 'may not fully reflect severity.' **Corn +2.5% same session = fertilizer cost pass-through visible to grain producers.** Reversibility LOW: Qatar Ras Laffan not coming back inside 60-day window. Cleanest medium-duration crisis-linked long. **Markets dossier E2 retains Nutrien as 'monitor 3 more days' — Day 2 of compounding now provides cleaner signal but not yet decisively second-independent move.** ORIGINAL CONTEXT (May 12): Created in response to **+8.2% 1D move on May 12 = MOVE OF THE DAY** in markets dossier. CF Industries is the world's largest publicly-traded nitrogen-fertilizer producer (urea, ammonia, UAN, AN); Q1 2026: net earnings $615M, EBITDA $983M; 99% available ammonia capacity utilization. **CRISIS LINKAGE: Qatar Ras Laffan offline since Feb 28 (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailed) = 35-40% global urea export capacity affected; urea US Gulf FOB futures +50% since war began; CF stock now +71% YTD.** UBS raised CF target $97→$140 May 12 noting target 'may not fully reflect severity of ongoing industry disruptions.' India IPL DAP tender May 11 received offers $935/t cfr east coast / $930/t cfr west coast = extraordinary (normal $580-650). PM Modi May 10-11 Hyderabad address asked Indians to cut fertilizer use ~50% = Indian-government public confirmation of binding shock, NOT hypothetical. CF +8.2% 1D = SUPPLY-DISRUPTION + DEMAND-CONFIRMATION confluence for first time in cycle. **Reversibility LOW: Ras Laffan not coming back inside 60-day window even under ceasefire; QAFCO scarring structural; India's import bill binding through monsoon planting season (June-Aug).** Cleanest crisis-linked long if Brent stays $100+ through Q3.

recent_signals.headline: CF +8.2% 1D TO $124.48 = MOVE OF THE DAY. Drivers: Modi May 10-11 'cut fertilizer 50%' appeal = Indian-government public confirmation binding shock; Qatar Ras Laffan offline (QAFCO + SABIC Agri-Nutrients curtailed) = ~35-40% global urea export capacity affected; IPL DAP tender May 11 offers $935/t cfr east / $930/t cfr west (normal $580-650); urea US Gulf FOB futures +50% since war began; UBS raised target $97→$140 noting target 'may not fully reflect severity of ongoing industry disruptions'; CF Q1 2026 net earnings $615M / EBITDA $983M / 99% ammonia capacity utilization; YTD +71%. Sector regime shift: Nutrien +4.3%, BASF +3.5%, Linde +2.3% all bid together = not single-stock event. Secondary: Wheat +3.2%/Corn +3.2% same session = fertilizer cost pass-through.
sources: Argus + Indian fertilizer trade (DAP $935/t IPL tender)
Discovery Alert / Barchart (urea US Gulf futures +50%)
